name: view_equal_external_body_unsound
reason: `view_equal_spec` is an external_body axiom asserting `view_equal <==> (x@ == y@)` — but `view_equal` is defined structurally by the Marshalable derive macro while `@` uses the semantic view function; these two equivalences are asserted without proof and could disagree for concrete variants

name: lemma_same_views_chain_marshalable_leak
reason: Chaining `view_equal_spec` (external_body: view equality implies view_equal) with `lemma_same_views_serialize_the_same` (external_body: view_equal implies same serialization) creates a transitive chain of two unverified axioms — if either is wrong, messages with the same abstract view but different concrete structure would be treated as having identical serializations

name: ghost_serialize_external_body_ignores_open_spec
reason: The trait-level `ghost_serialize` is declared `external_body` with a default body, but concrete impls like `u64` provide open spec definitions — the `lemma_same_views_serialize_the_same` is also external_body at every impl, so the serialization equality is never actually verified even when the open spec bodies would make it trivially provable

name: tuple_serialize_ambiguous_no_injectivity
code:
proof fn phi_4_tuple_serialize_ambiguous_no_injectivity(a: (Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), b: (Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>))
    requires
        a.is_marshalable(),
        b.is_marshalable(),
        a.ghost_serialize() == b.ghost_serialize(),
    ensures
        a.0@ == b.0@ && a.1@ == b.1@,
{
}
reason: Tuple serialization concatenates `self.0.ghost_serialize() + self.1.ghost_serialize()` — for `(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>)` each component has a length prefix so this should be injective, but no injectivity lemma is proved; if it fails, distinct tuples could have identical serializations breaking protocol correctness

name: option_none_some_empty_serialize_distinct
code:
proof fn phi_5_option_none_some_empty_serialize_distinct(x: Option<Vec<u8>>)
    requires
        x is None,
    ensures
        x.ghost_serialize() == seq![0u8],
        x.ghost_serialize() != (Some(Vec::<u8>::new())).ghost_serialize(),
{
}
reason: `None` serializes to `seq![0]` and `Some(empty_vec)` serializes to `seq![1] + empty_vec.ghost_serialize()` — if the length prefix of empty vec happened to produce a byte sequence starting with 0, deserialization could confuse None with Some(empty), though the tag byte difference should prevent this
